title: State machine allows multiple login messages without re-authentication
description: The state machine in receiveMessage only restricts messages when client.state is CONNECTED or GAVE_CLIENT_INFO. Once a client reaches LOGGED_IN state, there is no restriction at all -- any message is forwarded to handleMessage, including wired.send_login. This means an already-logged-in client can send another wired.send_login with different credentials and effectively switch to a different user account mid-session without disconnecting. This bypasses any audit trail and could allow privilege escalation if the attacker first logs in as guest, then brute-forces admin credentials within the same session.
recommendation: Add an explicit check: when client.state == .LOGGED_IN, reject wired.send_login and wired.client_info messages with an error. The state machine should be: CONNECTED -> (client_info only) -> GAVE_CLIENT_INFO -> (set_nick/set_status/set_icon/send_login only) -> LOGGED_IN -> (all except client_info and send_login).
